title: Surfacea racemosa
intro: Surfacea racemosa, also known as Surfacea racemosa, is a plant species native to South America, whose aerial parts are used for medicinal purposes. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of fever, colds, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Surfacea racemosa include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. taxonomy: Surfacea racemosa is classified as a species within the family Annonaceae, which includes over 160 genera of flowering plants. This family is part of the order Magnoliales, a group of flowering plants that are primarily found in tropical and subtropical regions.
